interpretation: The curve is broadly upward-sloping but exhibits a notable hump: the 2Y yield (11.95%) exceeds the 5Y (10.82%) and 10Y (11.15%), while the 364D dips below 182D, signaling distortions. The 2Y anomaly likely reflects tight banking sector liquidity and aggressive T-bill rollover pressure. With the repo rate at 10% and short-end yields near/above policy rates, BB's contractionary stance to combat persistent inflation (hovering ~10%) is evident. The steep long end (15Y at 12.68%) prices in sustained fiscal borrowing and inflation risk.
implications: Elevated 2Y yields suggest acute liquidity stress in the banking sector, with banks demanding premium for intermediate-term government lending over deploying funds in credit markets
BB is unlikely to ease the repo rate soon given short-end yields anchored near 10% and persistent inflationary pressures, maintaining a tight monetary corridor
The steep 10Y-20Y segment signals market expectations of heavy long-term government borrowing to finance fiscal deficits, embedding a significant term and inflation risk premium
